summary: Routing, new-tab behavior, visual regression coverage, and the required command gates are all green, but the accessibility part of the scope is still short. The new media links in `src/features/public-content/components/public-showroom.tsx` use repeated generic `aria-label`s instead of the corresponding note/link title, so assistive-tech users get bolt-on duplicate links rather than a natural extension of the existing title destinations.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Published note preview images route to the same note detail page as the note title.
Published link media opens the same external destination in a new tab.
The showroom appears visually stable on desktop and mobile under the existing screenshot/density coverage.
`npm run test:e2e -- --grep public-showroom` passed in the provided deterministic check run.
`npm run verify` passed in the provided deterministic check run.
missing_requirements: Accessibility alignment is incomplete: the media links at `src/features/public-content/components/public-showroom.tsx:103-108` and `src/features/public-content/components/public-showroom.tsx:156-163` expose generic accessible names instead of the item-specific title/destination, which does not satisfy the task's accessibility-alignment intent or the evaluator note about feeling like a natural extension of existing card behavior.
human_review_triggers: Keyboard/screen-reader navigation across the public showroom will hit multiple identical media-link names ('Open published note preview' / 'Open published link preview in a new tab') with no item-specific context.
